text:
Hey, how's it going man?
Good, good.
Where in the world are you?
San Francisco.
Nice.
You haven't made your way to the sunnier climbs just yet.
I actually will be in the British Virgin Islands in January, so in that way very much on the docket.
Sick.
Sick.
Dude, that's good, man.
With your partner.
Going sailing with some friends of mine.
Nice, nice, nice.
Dude, that's awesome, man.
Yeah.
This weather in New York sucks.
I can't imagine SF is, you know, as bad as here, but still nice to get out.
Yeah, you know, I'm usually in New York in December or January, so I feel like last few years that's all I've known of it.
So you very much have my sympathy for whatever that's worth.
Oh yeah.
I, I have become even more homebound.
I didn't know that was possible but you know, just give me a, another winter and I'm going to be like that old dude at the start of up just you know, yelling at the clouds and the dogs and little, little boy Scouts.
But yeah, man, things have been, things have been going interesting.
They've been going very interestingly.
When last we spoke we were spinning up this hiring side solution.
Had some like notions around what, where PMF might lie.
Since then what we've gone on to do is build out what is effectively an ATS system.
So you give out a link.
People apply by using Career Span.
So instead of filling out a form you have those like self reflection conversations with the AI and if you have two of them you qualify to apply for jobs on the Career Span network.
And then you can, you can do as many as you'd like but you use that to apply to jobs.
Got about a dozen jobs on it so far.
The first thing I wanted to discuss was we just signed a partnership with Marv Adventures.
They have about 100 or so companies or maybe 50 or so companies that are part of their stable of like part of their founder community.
We would want to identify those companies and basically just run polls of their job boards.
That would just be like a fixed number of companies on an ongoing basis.
We just get like data from them that can just stream straight to ilsa.
That would be, that would be absolutely cool.
I don't know if we're going to get all 50.
Maybe we're going to have to.
Why is this camera so weird?
Maybe we're going to have to.
What do you call it?
Yeah, anyway, I have to like screw around a little bit here and there, but that's roughly one area of need and Then the other sort of just personal area of need was wanting to like chat through with you.
We are actually getting more promising indications from people wanting to acquire us than invest in us.
Like by far got almost four or five companies that are in some capacity talking to us about an acquisition.
So it's, it's like a little bit of a strange experience.
I don't, I feel like I remember you said you'd exited before, right?
No, I mean I had a consulting business before SourceTech, but it was just sort of selling my time.
It didn't, it didn't exit as much as I turned it off so that I could be full time on Source Tech.
I will say I think the space is pretty acquisitive right now.
We have two customers that are being acquired, which is sort of a funny way to have churn happen.
But yeah, in both cases the acquirers are now customers.
So it's sort of like a nice.
Nice rollover in that way.
Nice.
Yeah, I mean that's the thing, right.
And I think we are in this sort of interesting space where we're collecting data no one else is.
We're approaching it in a way that few others are.
I think Jack and Jill and Dex are a lot closer to our paradigm now than anyone has been in the past.
We're still overtly more coaching and self reflection oriented than just, you know, have a chat with an AI.
But yeah, so we've got a couple of companies that are interested in us slash, you know, folks that, that would want to have that conversation with us, you know, by all means, you know, encourage you to guess in touch with them.
But then the, the, in the meantime, in order to best position us for an acquisition, what we're thinking is how do we create like froth, right.
Or create sort of like energy around the startup.
And so we're thinking, okay, can we get this flood of new roles in that will populate our job board that will incentivize folks to apply via career span and apply to these different roles and then we can, you know, there's going to involve some legwork and taking these candidates to these employers and you know, happy to do that but if we can get that sort of process going in the next three or four months and be really lean about it, we could probably sell for you know, 3 million hopefully and I'll walk away with like a couple hundred thousand, you know, and the investors are made whole and stuff like that.
I don't know if that sounds like two pie in the sky or if that is like an actually achievable thing in the context of tech.
I mean, I don't know what the prices of the acquisitions in the market look like right now.
Yeah, okay, fair enough.
You know, without saying too much, you know, one of them, they were doing okay and I think they were mostly bought for technology and team.
The other one is doing quite well and I think was bought for traction.
But I don't know any of the numbers really, other than how much data they consumed.
Yeah, no, even numbers aside, I think.
But I think you answered that question at the outset.
Right, by saying there's a lot of interest in the space and there's a lot of folks that are peeking around.
I don't know if this would be helpful.
This just popped into my head.
Do you know Peter Weddle of the TA Talent Network or the TA Talent Association?
I know of him, but I don't have a working relationship with him.
I don't know if it would be valuable for you to connect with him.
He's always looking for folks that are in talent and is always appreciative and you're a nice guy.
You'll make me look good by referring you.
So I don't know if that would be helpful, but he has been super helpful to us.
So I would just say if that's something you're open to, I would be happy to make that connection.
But he's, he's another one that I've reached out to around like, hey, who else in the space is, you know, like, I think speaking to folks that are kind of have that interesting POV that folks like yourself do is valuable to us.
Yeah, I mean, I'm happy to chat with him if it makes sense and I can put together a blurb when we get off just sort of about who I am and what we do.
Yeah.
You know, you're thinking about sort of who might this type of product complement?
Well, I would say broadly.
I think the sector that's most interested would be kind of the last generation of software tools for job seekers, which more specifically is resume builders.
There are hundreds of resume builder products and they are scared because you can just sort of one shot, spin up a resume now and it undoes like the WYSIWYG builders they've been doing for years.
But I don't, I don't know very many of them very well, in part because it's kind of hard to sell jobs data to them unless they're already committed to sort of building out that new product.
Right, right, right, right.
No, that Makes total sense.
I. Yeah, I, I would not be averse to selling to one of those companies.
I just wonder on some level, like, are they open to it even?
But you're right, they should be scared.
I think they should be scared.
And ATS companies should be scared.
Right.
I think either of them should be interested in buying something that is purporting to address that issue in a concrete way.
Yeah, I mean, I think, I think as a start, like, I can get you the names of all of the, the McKinsey Companies.
I'm pretty sure I could pull it from Getro right now, but I'll take my time and get you that and probably be around 50 or so companies.
If there was like a fixed amount or whatever that we could pay, just like monitor them and get like a data dump, that would be awesome.
So is get your end of life in their jobs data products?
I think so.
I saw that announcement.
I was gonna go watch the.
I was gonna go watch the seminar to find out.
Yeah.
Actually, that's funnily enough.
I don't know what Marvin's gonna do if Getro does sunset their product because they just paid Getra a lot of money to have that job board for a year.
You know, it's funny, I've talked to their CEO a bunch of times and back in the day we had discussed being sort of the data layer for their product.
And then after getting pretty far into that conversation, he spun up a competitive we're going to sell data now business.
So we had this big falling out and after they got acquired, I sent him an email just like, hey, man, congratulations.
And I haven't heard back.
So I sort of wonder if, you know, he's happy with how things played out.
But, I mean, they had no TAM to speak of.
Like, it was, it was just, it was like a playdoh.
It was like a land grab.
And then let's see what happens, right?
Surely.
But that's a shitty move, man.
I'm sorry that that happened to you.
You don't seem to like, be bothered by it, so I don't think you're, you're too upset, but.
Yeah, I'm not, I'm not meaning to speak ill.
I mean, we didn't totally.
Yeah.
Any agreements, any NDA, like, you know, they're free to do what they want.
Totally.
And I mean, it sounds like they found an acquirer that was a good fit.
Yeah.
Sort of a brief look and said, you know, oh, well, if they're going to do this sort of candidate sourcing use case by all means.
You know, that's not something I want to be a part of.
Well, my, my understanding was right before this acquisition news came out, they were trying to get an ATS off the ground.
So I mean, it's like, like what is the equivalent of evolution converging towards crabs but for bad HR tech, right?
Like the same design patterns repeat themselves of like, let's use the resume.
You know, let's do, you know, let's, let's give people a profile.
Like, it's all just the wrong, it's all so wrong answers only all the time.
But yeah.
I actually have a dear friend who spun up a startup building an ATS for four or five minutes and then she just sort of shrugged her shoulders and said, I don't like doing this, I don't want to do it.
And that's partially why we're trying to sell man is like the, the team is so drained.
I'm drained of having a drained team.
Like, it's just, you know, HR is a, is a brutal game.
It's, it's really not.
Yeah, it's, it's a, I don't know.
I, I, I've certainly found that it requires, I have a lot more respect for folks in this space than I think I did when I, when I started.
It is not an easy, easy place to carve out any level of like turf on in.
Yeah man, I, yeah, exactly.
Well, I, so I will tell you what, I will get you this list of places and you could let me know how that sort of setup would work for data dump.
I think we're pretty much certainly going to go ahead with that because at minimum even I can use some tools that I have to use that to like quickly populate accounts on notion and just get our job board to be looking good and a little beefed up.
So I'll get you that and then.
Yeah, in the meantime, I guess the only ask would be like if any of the various things and I'll enumerate them in the email.
But if any of these various things or folks that are relevant come to mind, just, you know, pop them my way or give me a heads up.
I would love to be able to connect.
Yeah, I'm not sure I would have anyone kind of teed up to intro, but keep my eyes open if I can.
Yeah, no sweat.
Yeah, it's all I could ever ask for.
So.
I've enjoyed the kind of here and there overlap that we have and I'm confident you guys are going to find your way with sort of the next development of the product, the next stage of it.
Thank you, man.
Thank you.
Yeah, I'm feeling strong about it.
Let's see how the next month goes.
But yeah, thank you.
Thank you again for jumping on and yeah, if I can ever be helpful, let me know.
And if you're in New York, let me know.
Yeah, I think I will be around kind of the 12th of January.
I have.
So I'm doing kind of a multi stage flight to get to BVI and so part of that I'll be spending some time with my brothers in New York, but I'm, I bought my BVI flights but from New York, but I haven't bought my flight to New York yet.
So at some point around the 12th of January I'll be around if you want to grab lunch or something.
Yeah, man, that would be awesome.
Shoot me a message.
Do you have my number?
I don't know.
7, 8, 6 9.
Setting in chat 3264.
Shoot me a.
Shoot me a message.
But yeah, that sounds awesome, man.
Love to get, love to get lunch.
Yeah.
Awesome.
All right, well I can take a look at.
If you want to just send over the, the list of portfolio companies with Marvin and you know, we can set up a data feed or I can show you how to make that API call.
Whatever works best.
Yeah, sounds good, man.
Perfect.
Thanks a bunch.
Well, good chatting with you as always and yeah, I guess I'll see you shortly.
Shortly, man, maybe we will.
All right, let you get back to your afternoon.
Cheers.
Cheers.
Bye.

utterances.text: It was like a land grab.

utterances.text: And then let's see what happens, right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Surely.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But that's a shitty move, man.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I'm sorry that that happened to you.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: You don't seem to like, be bothered by it, so I don't think you're, you're too upset, but.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Yeah, I'm not, I'm not meaning to speak ill.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: I mean, we didn't totally.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Any agreements, any NDA, like, you know, they're free to do what they want.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Totally.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: And I mean, it sounds like they found an acquirer that was a good fit.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Sort of a brief look and said, you know, oh, well, if they're going to do this sort of candidate sourcing use case by all means.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: You know, that's not something I want to be a part of.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Well, my, my understanding was right before this acquisition news came out, they were trying to get an ATS off the ground.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So I mean, it's like, like what is the equivalent of evolution converging towards crabs but for bad HR tech, right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like the same design patterns repeat themselves of like, let's use the resume.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: You know, let's do, you know, let's, let's give people a profile.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like, it's all just the wrong, it's all so wrong answers only all the time.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But yeah.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: I actually have a dear friend who spun up a startup building an ATS for four or five minutes and then she just sort of shrugged her shoulders and said, I don't like doing this, I don't want to do it.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And that's partially why we're trying to sell man is like the, the team is so drained.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I'm drained of having a drained team.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like, it's just, you know, HR is a, is a brutal game.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: It's, it's really not.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, it's, it's a, I don't know.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I, I, I've certainly found that it requires, I have a lot more respect for folks in this space than I think I did when I, when I started.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: It is not an easy, easy place to carve out any level of like turf on in.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah man, I, yeah, exactly.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Well, I, so I will tell you what, I will get you this list of places and you could let me know how that sort of setup would work for data dump.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think we're pretty much certainly going to go ahead with that because at minimum even I can use some tools that I have to use that to like quickly populate accounts on notion and just get our job board to be looking good and a little beefed up.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So I'll get you that and then.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, in the meantime, I guess the only ask would be like if any of the various things and I'll enumerate them in the email.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But if any of these various things or folks that are relevant come to mind, just, you know, pop them my way or give me a heads up.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I would love to be able to connect.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Yeah, I'm not sure I would have anyone kind of teed up to intro, but keep my eyes open if I can.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, no sweat.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, it's all I could ever ask for.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: I've enjoyed the kind of here and there overlap that we have and I'm confident you guys are going to find your way with sort of the next development of the product, the next stage of it.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Thank you, man.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, I'm feeling strong about it.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Let's see how the next month goes.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But yeah, thank you.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Thank you again for jumping on and yeah, if I can ever be helpful, let me know.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And if you're in New York, let me know.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Yeah, I think I will be around kind of the 12th of January.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: I have.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: So I'm doing kind of a multi stage flight to get to BVI and so part of that I'll be spending some time with my brothers in New York, but I'm, I bought my BVI flights but from New York, but I haven't bought my flight to New York yet.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: So at some point around the 12th of January I'll be around if you want to grab lunch or something.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, man, that would be awesome.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Shoot me a message.

utterances.text: Do you have my number?

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: I don't know.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Shoot me a.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Shoot me a message.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But yeah, that sounds awesome, man.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Love to get, love to get lunch.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Awesome.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: All right, well I can take a look at.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: If you want to just send over the, the list of portfolio companies with Marvin and you know, we can set up a data feed or I can show you how to make that API call.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Whatever works best.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, sounds good, man.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Perfect.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Thanks a bunch.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Well, good chatting with you as always and yeah, I guess I'll see you shortly.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Shortly, man, maybe we will.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: All right, let you get back to your afternoon.

utterances.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
utterances.text: Cheers.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Cheers.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Bye.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Hey, how's it going man? Good, good. Where in the world are you?

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: San Francisco.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Nice. You haven't made your way to the sunnier climbs just yet.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: I actually will be in the British Virgin Islands in January, so in that way very much on the docket.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Sick. Sick. Dude, that's good, man. With your partner.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: Going sailing with some friends of mine.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Nice, nice, nice. Dude, that's awesome, man. Yeah. This weather in New York sucks. I can't imagine SF is, you know, as bad as here, but still nice to get out.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: Yeah, you know, I'm usually in New York in December or January, so I feel like last few years that's all I've known of it. So you very much have my sympathy for whatever that's worth.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Oh yeah. I, I have become even more homebound. I didn't know that was possible but you know, just give me a, another winter and I'm going to be like that old dude at the start of up just you know, yelling at the clouds and the dogs and little, little boy Scouts. But yeah, man, things have been, things have been going interesting. They've been going very interestingly. When last we spoke we were spinning up this hiring side solution. Had some like notions around what, where PMF might lie. Since then what we've gone on to do is build out what is effectively an ATS system. So you give out a link. People apply by using Career Span. So instead of filling out a form you have those like self reflection conversations with the AI and if you have two of them you qualify to apply for jobs on the Career Span network. And then you can, you can do as many as you'd like but you use that to apply to jobs. Got about a dozen jobs on it so far. The first thing I wanted to discuss was we just signed a partnership with Marv Adventures. They have about 100 or so companies or maybe 50 or so companies that are part of their stable of like part of their founder community. We would want to identify those companies and basically just run polls of their job boards. That would just be like a fixed number of companies on an ongoing basis. We just get like data from them that can just stream straight to ilsa. That would be, that would be absolutely cool. I don't know if we're going to get all 50. Maybe we're going to have to. Why is this camera so weird? Maybe we're going to have to. What do you call it? Yeah, anyway, I have to like screw around a little bit here and there, but that's roughly one area of need and Then the other sort of just personal area of need was wanting to like chat through with you. We are actually getting more promising indications from people wanting to acquire us than invest in us. Like by far got almost four or five companies that are in some capacity talking to us about an acquisition. So it's, it's like a little bit of a strange experience. I don't, I feel like I remember you said you'd exited before, right?

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: No, I mean I had a consulting business before SourceTech, but it was just sort of selling my time. It didn't, it didn't exit as much as I turned it off so that I could be full time on Source Tech. I will say I think the space is pretty acquisitive right now. We have two customers that are being acquired, which is sort of a funny way to have churn happen. But yeah, in both cases the acquirers are now customers.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: So it's sort of like a nice.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: Nice rollover in that way.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Nice. Yeah, I mean that's the thing, right. And I think we are in this sort of interesting space where we're collecting data no one else is. We're approaching it in a way that few others are. I think Jack and Jill and Dex are a lot closer to our paradigm now than anyone has been in the past. We're still overtly more coaching and self reflection oriented than just, you know, have a chat with an AI. But yeah, so we've got a couple of companies that are interested in us slash, you know, folks that, that would want to have that conversation with us, you know, by all means, you know, encourage you to guess in touch with them. But then the, the, in the meantime, in order to best position us for an acquisition, what we're thinking is how do we create like froth, right. Or create sort of like energy around the startup. And so we're thinking, okay, can we get this flood of new roles in that will populate our job board that will incentivize folks to apply via career span and apply to these different roles and then we can, you know, there's going to involve some legwork and taking these candidates to these employers and you know, happy to do that but if we can get that sort of process going in the next three or four months and be really lean about it, we could probably sell for you know, 3 million hopefully and I'll walk away with like a couple hundred thousand, you know, and the investors are made whole and stuff like that. I don't know if that sounds like two pie in the sky or if that is like an actually achievable thing in the context of tech.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: I mean, I don't know what the prices of the acquisitions in the market look like right now.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Yeah, okay, fair enough.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: You know, without saying too much, you know, one of them, they were doing okay and I think they were mostly bought for technology and team. The other one is doing quite well and I think was bought for traction. But I don't know any of the numbers really, other than how much data they consumed.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Yeah, no, even numbers aside, I think. But I think you answered that question at the outset. Right, by saying there's a lot of interest in the space and there's a lot of folks that are peeking around. I don't know if this would be helpful. This just popped into my head. Do you know Peter Weddle of the TA Talent Network or the TA Talent Association?

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: I know of him, but I don't have a working relationship with him.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: I don't know if it would be valuable for you to connect with him. He's always looking for folks that are in talent and is always appreciative and you're a nice guy. You'll make me look good by referring you. So I don't know if that would be helpful, but he has been super helpful to us. So I would just say if that's something you're open to, I would be happy to make that connection. But he's, he's another one that I've reached out to around like, hey, who else in the space is, you know, like, I think speaking to folks that are kind of have that interesting POV that folks like yourself do is valuable to us.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: Yeah, I mean, I'm happy to chat with him if it makes sense and I can put together a blurb when we get off just sort of about who I am and what we do.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Yeah.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: You know, you're thinking about sort of who might this type of product complement? Well, I would say broadly. I think the sector that's most interested would be kind of the last generation of software tools for job seekers, which more specifically is resume builders. There are hundreds of resume builder products and they are scared because you can just sort of one shot, spin up a resume now and it undoes like the WYSIWYG builders they've been doing for years. But I don't, I don't know very many of them very well, in part because it's kind of hard to sell jobs data to them unless they're already committed to sort of building out that new product.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Right, right, right, right. No, that Makes total sense. I. Yeah, I, I would not be averse to selling to one of those companies. I just wonder on some level, like, are they open to it even? But you're right, they should be scared. I think they should be scared. And ATS companies should be scared. Right. I think either of them should be interested in buying something that is purporting to address that issue in a concrete way. Yeah, I mean, I think, I think as a start, like, I can get you the names of all of the, the McKinsey Companies. I'm pretty sure I could pull it from Getro right now, but I'll take my time and get you that and probably be around 50 or so companies. If there was like a fixed amount or whatever that we could pay, just like monitor them and get like a data dump, that would be awesome.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: So is get your end of life in their jobs data products?

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: I think so. I saw that announcement. I was gonna go watch the. I was gonna go watch the seminar to find out. Yeah. Actually, that's funnily enough. I don't know what Marvin's gonna do if Getro does sunset their product because they just paid Getra a lot of money to have that job board for a year.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: You know, it's funny, I've talked to their CEO a bunch of times and back in the day we had discussed being sort of the data layer for their product. And then after getting pretty far into that conversation, he spun up a competitive we're going to sell data now business. So we had this big falling out and after they got acquired, I sent him an email just like, hey, man, congratulations. And I haven't heard back. So I sort of wonder if, you know, he's happy with how things played out.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: But, I mean, they had no TAM to speak of. Like, it was, it was just, it was like a playdoh. It was like a land grab. And then let's see what happens, right? Surely. But that's a shitty move, man. I'm sorry that that happened to you. You don't seem to like, be bothered by it, so I don't think you're, you're too upset, but.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: Yeah, I'm not, I'm not meaning to speak ill. I mean, we didn't totally.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Yeah.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: Any agreements, any NDA, like, you know, they're free to do what they want.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Totally.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: And I mean, it sounds like they found an acquirer that was a good fit.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Yeah.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: Sort of a brief look and said, you know, oh, well, if they're going to do this sort of candidate sourcing use case by all means. You know, that's not something I want to be a part of.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Well, my, my understanding was right before this acquisition news came out, they were trying to get an ATS off the ground. So I mean, it's like, like what is the equivalent of evolution converging towards crabs but for bad HR tech, right? Like the same design patterns repeat themselves of like, let's use the resume. You know, let's do, you know, let's, let's give people a profile. Like, it's all just the wrong, it's all so wrong answers only all the time. But yeah.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: I actually have a dear friend who spun up a startup building an ATS for four or five minutes and then she just sort of shrugged her shoulders and said, I don't like doing this, I don't want to do it.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: And that's partially why we're trying to sell man is like the, the team is so drained. I'm drained of having a drained team. Like, it's just, you know, HR is a, is a brutal game. It's, it's really not. Yeah, it's, it's a, I don't know. I, I, I've certainly found that it requires, I have a lot more respect for folks in this space than I think I did when I, when I started. It is not an easy, easy place to carve out any level of like turf on in. Yeah man, I, yeah, exactly. Well, I, so I will tell you what, I will get you this list of places and you could let me know how that sort of setup would work for data dump. I think we're pretty much certainly going to go ahead with that because at minimum even I can use some tools that I have to use that to like quickly populate accounts on notion and just get our job board to be looking good and a little beefed up. So I'll get you that and then. Yeah, in the meantime, I guess the only ask would be like if any of the various things and I'll enumerate them in the email. But if any of these various things or folks that are relevant come to mind, just, you know, pop them my way or give me a heads up. I would love to be able to connect.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: Yeah, I'm not sure I would have anyone kind of teed up to intro, but keep my eyes open if I can.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Yeah, no sweat. Yeah, it's all I could ever ask for. So.

chunks.speaker: Alec Barrett-Wilsdon
chunks.text: I've enjoyed the kind of here and there overlap that we have and I'm confident you guys are going to find your way with sort of the next development of the product, the next stage of it.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Thank you, man. Thank you. Yeah, I'm feeling strong about it. Let's see how the next month goes. But yeah, thank you. Thank you again for jumping on and yeah, if I can ever be helpful, let me know. And if you're in New York, let me know.

chunks.text: Yeah, I think I will be around kind of the 12th of January. I have. So at some point around the 12th of January I'll be around if you want to grab lunch or something.

chunks.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
chunks.text: Yeah, man, that would be awesome. Shoot me a message. Do you have my number?
